message:
fix(serve/test,tui/dashboard): align OIDC test harness with (claims, err) return + add missing io import

Two follow-ups from the earlier "fix(serve): propagate auth claims
through middleware and pick highest realm_access role" commit
(6090f111) that taught validateOIDCToken to return
`(map[string]interface{}, error)` instead of a bare `error`.

internal/serve/handler_branch_test.go
internal/serve/server_test.go

- Every test site that bound `err := s.validateOIDCToken(...)`
  or `err := s.authenticateOIDC(...)` is updated to
  `_, err := ...` because the function now returns two values.
  Affected tests include:
    TestValidateOIDCToken_IncompleteConfig
    TestValidateOIDCToken_UnsupportedAlg
    TestValidateOIDCToken_InvalidIssuer
    TestValidateOIDCToken_InvalidAudience
    TestValidateOIDCToken_ExpiredToken
    TestValidateOIDCToken_NotYetValid
    TestValidateOIDCToken_JWKSFetchError
    TestValidateOIDCToken_JWKSKidNotFound
  plus the matching error-path cases in server_test.go that
  exercise authenticateOIDC via the middleware path.
- Each affected call continues to ignore the claims map and
  only asserts on the error text, since these tests are
  specifically error-path coverage.  No new assertions were
  added for the returned claims; those belong in a dedicated
  positive-path test.

internal/tui/dashboard/local_perf.go

- Add `"io"` to the import block.  An earlier edit introduced a
  use of io.Copy or io.ReadAll in this file without updating the
  import list, so `go build ./...` was failing with
  "undefined: io".  This is a one-line fix that restores the
  build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
